input_ontology.justification: Task taxonomy is US-edtech-centric; poorly covers CHW triage categories.
evidence_quotes: [Q1] 'The task taxonomy covers five categories common in US edtech.' (p.1)
information_gaps: No mapping between benchmark tasks and CHW triage workflow.

input_content.justification: Prompts drawn from US undergraduate materials; irrelevant to CHW public-health context.
evidence_quotes: [Q2] 'All prompts are drawn from US undergraduate coursework.' (p.1)

input_form.justification: Benchmark inputs are long-form text; CHW users send short voice-to-text transcripts.
evidence_quotes: [Q3] 'Inputs are UTF-8 text averaging ~1000 words per item.' (p.1)

output_ontology.justification: 5-way labels do not map to the 5 triage categories used by CHWs in practice.

output_content.justification: Ground-truth annotations from US annotators may reflect US clinical norms.
evidence_quotes: [Q5] 'Ground-truth labels were assigned by annotators based in the United States.' (p.2)

output_form.justification: Single categorical label output is compatible with mobile UX constraints.
evidence_quotes: [Q6] 'Each item returns a single categorical label.' (p.2)

overall_summary: DummyBench is not valid for CHW deployment in Kenya without substantial adaptation.
remediation_suggestions: Collect Swahili CHW prompts; re-annotate with Kenyan health workers; remap label set to MoH triage categories.
